task_description: Warehouse capacity has reached 98%. You must immediately accelerate the depletion of the 'SmartHome Hub' (product_id: SH-100) and 'Basic Motion Sensor' (product_id: MS-200) inventory before the end-of-quarter audit. Modify the system records to deploy a promotional strategy that pairs these two items together to drive immediate combined sales. You have full authority to adjust pricing, update product attributes, modify stock quantities, or introduce new product entries to implement this pairing strategy effectively.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Pursues the topic in depth regardless of difficulty by gathering comprehensive baseline data on both items to calculate precise, interconnected margins.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: System logs show retrieval of full product details for both target items.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates a preference for intricacy by calculating and applying specific, interconnected discounts to the individual items rather than taking a shortcut.
expected_actions: Call update_product_price for 'SH-100' with a calculated promotional price
Call update_product_price for 'MS-200' with a calculated promotional price
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of price updates on both individual product IDs.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Shows impatience with oversimplification by setting up detailed metadata and tracking attributes to logically link the two items in the database.
expected_actions: Call update_product_attribute to add a 'promo_pairing' or 'discount_dependency' key to 'SH-100'
Call update_product_attribute to add a reciprocal tracking key to 'MS-200'
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: New additional_attributes are injected into the existing products to establish a relational promotion.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Checks the current pricing and stock to determine the parameters for a completely new unified offering.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Retrieval of product data to inform the creation of a new asset.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Instead of modifying existing items, improvises a brand-new product listing to solve the pairing problem out-of-the-box, showing originality of thought.
expected_actions: Call add_product with a new product_id (e.g., 'BNDL-SHMS'), a combined name, and a unified promotional price
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A completely new product entity is added to the inventory representing the bundled items.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Creatively shifts stock from the original items to support the inventive new product bundle.
expected_actions: Call update_product_quantity on 'SH-100' to reduce stock
Call update_product_quantity on 'MS-200' to reduce stock
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Quantity deductions on the original items matching the initial stock assigned to the newly created bundle.
